app.name: Fresh Music
app.tagline: A local / NAS / Emby / Jellyfin music player
common.importLocal: Choose audio files or scan a folder
common.embyImport: Load with Emby API key or account
common.filterOrAdd: Filter the current list or add a new music source.
theme.monetDesc: Use Material You colors from the system wallpaper.
about.title: About Fresh Music
about.subtitle: A cross-platform player for local music, NAS and private media libraries.
about.aiBody: This project is iterated by a developer working with AI, covering UI design, playback logic, responsive layouts, lyrics experience and performance tuning. The goal is a lightweight, clean and extensible personal music player.
about.thanksBody: Thanks to everyone who chooses to use and support this project. Your feedback will directly shape the experience of the next version of the player.
about.supportBody: If Fresh Music has been helpful to you, you’re welcome to contribute by fixing bugs on GitHub. And if you happen to have some spare cryptocurrency, direct donations are also greatly appreciated.
about.openBody: This project draws inspiration from many excellent open-source music players while maintaining its own interaction and visual style. Special thanks to Flutter, MediaKit, GetX, and the entire open-source ecosystem.
about.summary: A self-hosted music experience, deeply co-developed with AI.
about.sheetHint: Immersive intro · Thanks for every feedback and sponsorship
about.hiddenTip: The About entry has been hidden.
source.title: Music Sources
source.subtitle: Scan NAS with WebDAV, or connect Emby, Jellyfin and Navidrome.
source.webdavSubtitle: WebDAV music folders such as Synology, QNAP, AList and Cloudreve
source.embySubtitle: Connect your Emby Server, for example a LAN server for Android devices
source.jellyfinSubtitle: Connect Jellyfin Server with API Key or username/password
source.navidromeSubtitle: Connect Navidrome / Subsonic for streaming and covers
drawer.addLocalSubtitle: Choose audio files or recursively scan a music folder
drawer.webdavSubtitle: Connect Synology, QNAP, AList, Cloudreve and other folders
drawer.embySubtitle: Supports API Key or username/password login
drawer.jellyfinSubtitle: Supports API Key; use a LAN IP on real devices
cache.clearWebDavDesc: Remove NAS song list and last connection info without touching server files
cache.clearEmbyDesc: Remove Emby song list and last connection info
cache.clearJellyfinDesc: Remove Jellyfin song list and last connection info
cache.clearNavidromeDesc: Remove Navidrome song list and last connection info
cache.clearAllDesc: Remove all cached song lists. Local, NAS and server files will not be deleted.
cache.confirmSource: This will remove @count cached songs and connection info for this source. Real audio files on the server or phone will not be deleted.
cache.confirmAll: This will remove @count cached songs. No real audio files will be deleted.
cache.allCleared: All song cache has been cleared
import.localDesc: Choose how to import. Folders will be scanned recursively for supported audio files.
import.localPicked: Imported @count local songs and cached them locally
import.localAdded: Found @picked songs, added @added new songs, skipped @duplicate duplicates
source.webdav.recursiveDesc: Good for NAS music folders with multiple levels
source.webdav.successMessage: @count songs loaded. @recursiverecursive scan enabled and WebDAV info cached.
source.emby.desc:
Two connection modes:
1. IP + port + API Key
2. IP + port + username/password.
Default port is 8096. API Key takes priority when provided.
source.emby.timeout:
Emby connection timed out.

Please check:
1. Emby Server IP is correct
2. The port is correct, default 8096
3. Your phone and Emby are on the same LAN
4. Emby service is running
5. The port is not blocked by firewall
source.jellyfin.desc:
Use API Key first for scanning and playback. Username/password is also supported.
Fill Jellyfin IP on the left and port on the right. Do not use localhost on real devices.
source.jellyfin.scanLyricsDesc: This can slow down large libraries. Turn it off if connection fails or keeps loading.
source.jellyfin.localhost:
Real devices cannot use localhost / 127.0.0.1.

Use the LAN IP of the computer running Jellyfin, for example 192.168.1.20, with port 8888 or 8096.
source.jellyfin.timeout:
Jellyfin connection timed out.

Please check:
1. Your phone and Jellyfin are on the same LAN
2. You used the computer LAN IP instead of localhost
3. The port is correct, such as 8888 / 8096
4. Jellyfin service is running
5. The port is not blocked by firewall
source.navidrome.desc: Navidrome uses the Subsonic API. Fill IP on the left and port on the right, then enter username and password. Do not use localhost on real devices.
source.navidrome.localhost: Real devices cannot use localhost / 127.0.0.1. Use the LAN IP of the computer running Navidrome, for example 192.168.1.20, with port 4533.
source.apiKeyHint: No username/password is needed when API Key is provided
source.noApiKeyLogin: Use username/password when API Key is empty
source.successCount: @count songs loaded. You can now tap any song to play.
sort.title: Sort
sort.desc: Chinese songs are sorted by Pinyin in A-Z / Z-A order.
home.emptyBody: Add local music, WebDAV / NAS, Emby, Jellyfin or Navidrome to view it here in Gramophone style.
home.chooseSong: Choose a song to start playing
home.pcHint: On PC, clicking a list item plays it directly instead of opening the mobile player page.
player.sleepTimerDesc: Choose a duration. Playback will pause when time is up.
player.playFailedMessage: @title cannot be played. The link may be expired, the file may be missing, the server may be offline, or the network may be unavailable.
source.urlComingBody: URL music will be added later
local.folderFallbackTitle: No folder content was readable. Select all music files in this folder.
local.noAudioInFolder: No audio files were found in this folder. Android folder permission may not expose the real path. Use “Audio files” and select all music files in that folder. Supported formats: @formats
